summary: Tested new admin endpoints, webhook safety, and /idcard route. All 15 backend pytest tests pass (root, staff CRUD, applications POST with empty webhook, admin login, admin applications, admin staff CRUD with token guards). Frontend Playwright tests verified: /idcard renders ID generator; old /id-card no longer matches a route; /admin login flow works (wrong token shows error toast, correct token enters console); Staff tab shows 8 seeded rows; Add/Edit/Delete staff flows work end-to-end including native confirm dialog; Applications tab lists existing TEST_ applications. Navbar 'ID Card' link points to /idcard.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminPage StaffManager.useEffect has empty dep array but uses `load` (which closes over setLoading/setStaff) — fine because state setters are stable, but ESLint exhaustive-deps would warn. Cosmetic only.
Footer still contains an 'Admin' link to /admin (visible in old /id-card body preview) — fine functionally; consider hiding if you don't want guests to discover the admin page surface.
Backend uses deprecated @app.on_event('startup'/'shutdown') — works but FastAPI recommends lifespan handlers.
Seed runs only when staff collection is empty — re-seed not idempotent if collection partially modified. Acceptable for MVP.
send_discord_application uses requests in executor — fine for fire-and-forget; consider httpx.AsyncClient for fully async path.
seed_data_creation: Test created and removed one staff (callsign EMS-PW1, name TEST_PlaywrightStaff*) via UI. Backend pytest creates+deletes its own TEST_ staff (EMS-TST). Several TEST_ applications remain in DB from earlier runs — harmless, only visible in admin console.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_pillbox_api.py now reads REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (env var not exported at shell level). Admin token header is X-Admin-Token. Old /id-card route intentionally removed (router warning 'No routes matched' is expected). DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL is empty — backend logs an info notice and continues; do not treat that as failure.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found in this iteration.
